content: Your ability to derive vitamin A from plant foods can be impacted by genetic variations, specifically mutations in the BCO1 gene.
Dr. Chris Masterjohn reports being heterozygous for two mutations in the BCO1 gene, reducing his ability to convert carotenoids to retinol by 25%.
To compensate for genetic limitations, individuals may need to consume 3 to 5,000 IU of retinol per day, primarily through liver intake or cod liver oil.
Other factors affecting vitamin A conversion include digestive issues, nutrient deficiencies, insufficient dietary fat, and certain health conditions such as thyroid problems.
